arguments.command: which dotnet addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; which readelf objdump 2>/dev/null

# Try downloading libmonosgen-2.0.so debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try the ELF buildid format on MSDL
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try alternate format with sym extension
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try _.debug format  
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try with dbg extension
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg" 2>&1

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to symbolicate the .NET runtime frames in this Android tombstone. The key library is `libmonosgen-2.0.so` with BuildId `1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be`. The frames are at offsets:

- #01 pc 0x222098
- #02 pc 0x2241ac
- #03 pc 0x1abc80
- #04 pc 0x1abd14
- #05 pc 0x16dfa4
- #06 pc 0x16db6c


- #07 pc 0x16de04

To resolve these, I need to fetch the debug symbols from Microsoft's symbol server using the BuildId, which I can do through their standard symbol server endpoint or debuginfod protocol.

Let me try constructing the correct URL path for fetching the ELF debug symbols using the build ID from Microsoft's server. I'm realizing I need to be practical about this—I don't have direct access to the specific debug symbols for this libmonosgen-2.0.so build, so let me try a few concrete approaches like checking Microsoft's symbol server directly, exploring NuGet packages that contain the .NET runtime, or using the debuginfod protocol with the build ID to fetch the symbols. I need to actually download the debug symbols to resolve these addresses to function names. The Microsoft symbol server uses a specific URL pattern for ELF files with the build ID, so let me try fetching from there. I could also use the `dotnet-symbol` tool if it's available, which would handle the download automatically. Let me attempt both approaches to see which one works.

arguments.command: which dotnet addr2line llvm-symbolizer llvm-addr2line 2>/dev/null; dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; which readelf objdump 2>/dev/null

# Try downloading libmonosgen-2.0.so debug symbols from Microsoft symbol server
# BuildId: 1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be
BUILDID="1eb39fc72918c7c6c0c610b79eb3d3d47b2f81be"
FILENAME="libmonosgen-2.0.so"

# Try the ELF buildid format on MSDL
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try alternate format with sym extension
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try _.debug format  
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/_.debug/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/_.debug" 2>&1
echo ""
# Try with dbg extension
curl -sSL -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" "https://msdl.microsoft.com/download/symbols/${FILENAME}.dbg/elf-buildid-sym-${BUILDID}/${FILENAME}.dbg" 2>&1
